---
title: Local Government Taxes by Type and Rurality, 2022
description: Horizontal faceted bar chart comparing 2022 local government tax revenue
  by tax type across rural and nonrural counties.
date: '2026-05-14'
layout: default
permalink: /charts/rwjf-seeds-of-change-public-funding-facet-taxes-by-rurality-horiz-bar/
categories: []
tags: []
image: 2022_facet_taxes_by_rurality_horiz_bar.png
chart:
  title: Local Government Taxes by Type and Rurality, 2022
  chartType: bar
  interactive: false
  source: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Economic Research Service
  sourceUrl: https://www.ers.usda.gov/data-products/county-typology-codes/
  featured: false
producedBy: rwjf-seeds-of-change-public-funding
usesDatasets:
  - census-of-governments
  - bls-cpi-deflators
  - usda-county-typology
usesPackages:
  - ruraldefinitions
usesResources: []
format:
  gfm:
    toc: false
    wrap: none
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

![Local Government Taxes by Type and Rurality, 2022](2022_facet_taxes_by_rurality_horiz_bar.png)

## Overview

Shows 2022 per-capita local government tax revenue broken out by tax type (property, sales/excise, individual income, corporate income) for rural vs. nonrural counties, enabling direct comparison of local revenue mix by geography.

## Key Findings

- Property taxes are the dominant local tax instrument in both rural and nonrural counties in 2022.
- Nonrural counties generate substantially more per-capita revenue from sales and income taxes than rural counties.
- Rural counties rely more heavily on property taxes as a share of total local tax revenue compared to nonrural counties.

## Reproducibility

Generated by `R/final_viz/G7_create_bar_chart_taxes_by_rurality.R` in the producing project.


::: {.callout-note}
## Dangling references

The following slugs are referenced by this project but do not yet have nodes in Dataverse. They are intentionally preserved as future content needs:

- `dataset/census-of-governments`
- `dataset/bls-cpi-deflators`
:::
